name: Yam Wunsen Sai Mu (Noodle Soup W/pork)
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Wunsen (cellophane noodles) -- soaked in warm water for about 15 minutes
1 cup Sliced phak bung (swamp cabbage) -- ordinary cabbage or kale will do as a replacement
2 Spring onions (green onions/scallions) thinly sliced -- including the green segment
1/4 cup Phak chi (whole coriander plant - including the root) -- chopped
1 tsp Prik Thai, about (ground black pepper)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the marinade: Mix fish sauce, Maggi sauce, minced garlic, Prik Thai (ground black pepper), and rice flour or cornstarch.
2. Combine ground pork with the marinade ingredients and form into small meatballs. Let marinate for 3-4 hours.
3. Soak and drain cellophane noodles as per package instructions.
4. Bring soup stock to a boil. Add sliced phak bung, spring onions, fish sauce, and phak chi. Cook until the meatballs are cooked through (they will float).
5. Remove from heat, transfer meatballs and vegetables to a serving bowl, and add the noodles. The near boiling soup cooks the noodles.
6. Serve with Thai condiments such as nam pla prik (chilies in fish sauce), prik dong (chilies in vinegar), sugar, prik phom (ground chilies), and ground peanuts.
